incorrects.description: Student selected 3 — likely subtracted 11 (Pretzels) from 14 (Crackers) instead of comparing to 8 (Popcorn)

steps.text: Let's think about this together. We're comparing Crackers and Popcorn. Count the symbols in each row.

incorrects.description: Student selected 14 — chose the total for Crackers instead of finding the difference

steps.text: Here's a hint: 14 is how many chose Crackers. But we need the difference—how many MORE than Popcorn.

incorrects.description: Student selected 22 — added 14 and 8 instead of subtracting to find the difference

steps.text: You're working on it. Here's what helps: to find how many MORE, we subtract the smaller number from the bigger number.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: System highlights Crackers row showing 14 symbols, Popcorn row showing 8 symbols, then demonstrates 14 minus 8 equals 6 with visual alignment

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Crackers has 14 symbols. Popcorn has 8 symbols. To find how many more, I subtract: 14 minus 8 equals 6. Six more students chose Crackers than Popcorn.
